title: Python, Tcl, Tkinter, Threads, and the Event Loop
description: The interface creates multiple roots, blocks callbacks with work, calls widgets from an arbitrary thread, and exits with scheduled callbacks still alive. Learners cumulatively produce single-root event-loop lifecycle contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace Python calls through tkinter into one Tcl interpreter and the Tk widget toolkit with recorded patch levels.
Create one application root, initialize state before mainloop, and shut down widgets and callbacks deliberately.
Keep event handlers short and divide long generation or search work into bounded event-loop turns.
